mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so declines in FEDFUNDS over recent months should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts are driven by acute recession panic and equities continue to sell off.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 1-3 month window but subsequent 20d SPX returns are negative in most triggered samples, or if the 20d hit rate is below 50% for two consecutive years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already high, further tight policy raises real financing costs and pressures valuation multiples, so SPX should fall over the next month after policy rates remain above the stated threshold. The signal fails if high rates coexist with strong liquidity-driven equity gains.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, SPX delivers positive 20d returns in more than 60% of cases or the average forward return is not below zero across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and softer demand, which should translate into lower S&P 500 prices over the next 60 trading days. The effect is stronger when UNRATE has been trending up for several months rather than just one print.
failure_condition: Reject if a 0.3pp or larger 3-month rise in UNRATE is followed by positive 60d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average 60d return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher payroll growth supports consumption and revenue expectations, so a sustained increase in PAYEMS should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll gains are accompanied by abrupt rate tightening that overwhelms demand support.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign of the relation turns persistently negative in any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth and housing starts together identify broad final-demand strength, so simultaneous improvements in RSAFS and HOUST should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Either series alone is insufficient; the joint confirmation is what supports the risk-on signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either single proxy alone, or if triggered cases produce negative average 20d SPX returns over a full market cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving output and earnings capacity, so a positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO should be followed by higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when production gains are driven by inventory restocking without broad demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not produce positive average 20d SPX returns, or if the return sign is negative in a majority of months where INDPRO is above its recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: If CPI inflation is falling while the policy rate remains elevated, real rates rise and financial conditions tighten, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL decelerates without a matching cut in FEDFUNDS.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL decelerates while FEDFUNDS > CPIAUCSL 3m annualized change
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with elevated FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average 5d return is not below zero in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, stable unemployment combined with steady retail sales indicates resilient consumption without labor-market deterioration, supporting equities over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism fails if demand stays firm but unemployment begins to rise sharply, signaling hidden recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with stable UNRATE and rising RSAFS do not deliver above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms random chance across a rolling 3-year evaluation.
